definition.zh: 元素通过改变不透明度出现或消失。
definition.en: An opacity transition brings an element into view or carries it out without changing its geometry.

definition.zh: 元素从屏幕外沿左、右、上或下方滑入。
definition.en: A positional transition moves content from beyond an edge into its resting location.

definition.zh: 元素出现时从较小尺寸放大到完整尺寸，通常与淡入搭配。
definition.en: An appearing element expands from a reduced scale to its final dimensions, commonly while opacity rises.

definition.zh: 元素出现时略微越过最终状态，再像回弹一样落到位。
definition.en: An entrance crosses the final scale briefly, then settles back to create a compact spring-like accent.

distinction.zh: 弹入会越过最终状态后回弹到位；缩放入场可以直接停在最终尺寸。
distinction.en: Pop in overshoots and settles; Scale in can stop directly at its final size.

definition.zh: 内容逐步显露，通常通过动画化裁切路径或遮罩实现。
definition.en: A moving clipping boundary or mask exposes content progressively.

definition.zh: 元素被添加到屏幕或从屏幕移除时播放的动画。
definition.en: Lifecycle motion communicates when an element joins or leaves the rendered interface.

distinction.zh: 进入 / 离开描述元素加入或移出界面时的生命周期动画；滑入只是其中一种带方向的实现。
distinction.en: Enter / Exit names the lifecycle motion used when an element is added or removed; Slide in is one directional implementation.

definition.zh: 动画中预先定义的节点（如 0%、50%、100%），浏览器会补全节点之间的画面。
definition.en: Named timeline checkpoints assign property values at selected offsets; interpolation determines the states between them.

definition.zh: 生成起始值与结束值之间的所有中间帧，让运动保持连续。
definition.en: A renderer calculates intermediate property values between defined states to produce continuous change.

distinction.zh: 插值 / 补间负责生成两个值之间的中间帧；关键帧负责定义时间线上的状态节点。
distinction.en: Interpolation / Tween generates frames between values; Keyframes defines state checkpoints on a timeline.

definition.zh: 让多个项目依次播放动画，并在相邻项目之间加入短延迟，形成级联节奏。
definition.en: Related items start at offset times, turning one transition into a readable sequence.

definition.zh: 有意识地安排多段动画的时间关系，让它们呈现为一个协调的整体动作。
definition.en: A coordinated timeline orders multiple motions so their starts, overlaps, and finishes express one event.

distinction.zh: 动效编排协调多段动画之间的时间关系；关键帧描述一段动画内部的状态。
distinction.en: Orchestration coordinates timing across multiple animations; Keyframes describes states inside one animation.

definition.zh: 动画开始前等待的时间。
definition.en: The delay is the interval between a trigger and the first active frame.

distinction.zh: 延迟是动画开始前的等待时间；时长是动画运行所花的时间。
distinction.en: Delay is the wait before motion starts; Duration is the time the motion spends running.

definition.zh: 一段动画完成所需的时间。
definition.en: Duration measures the elapsed time from an animation's start state to its end state.

definition.zh: 控制元素在动画开始前或结束后是否保留首帧或末帧样式，例如 forwards。
definition.en: A fill mode selects which keyframe styles apply outside the active animation interval.

distinction.zh: 填充模式控制动画有效区间外保留哪一帧的样式；关键帧定义有效区间内的状态。
distinction.en: Fill mode controls which styles persist outside the active interval; Keyframes defines states inside it.

definition.zh: 被划分为若干离散步骤的动画，例如倒计时器。
definition.en: Progress advances through fixed discrete states instead of continuously interpolated values.

distinction.zh: 步进动画在离散状态间跳变；常规关键帧通常会在状态之间连续插值。
distinction.en: Stepped animation jumps between discrete states; regular keyframes usually interpolate continuously between states.

definition.zh: 沿 X 轴或 Y 轴移动元素。
definition.en: A transform shifts visual coordinates horizontally, vertically, or both while leaving layout flow unchanged.

definition.zh: 放大或缩小元素。
definition.en: A transform multiplies an element's rendered width and height around a chosen origin.

distinction.zh: 缩放改变元素尺寸；位移改变元素位置。
distinction.en: Scale changes an element's size; Translate changes its position.

definition.zh: 让元素围绕一个点旋转。
definition.en: A transform changes an element's angular orientation around its transform origin.

distinction.zh: 旋转改变元素围绕锚点的角度；位移改变元素位置。
distinction.en: Rotate changes an element's angle around an anchor; Translate changes its position.

definition.zh: 沿 X 轴或 Y 轴倾斜元素，使其从矩形形态产生切变。
definition.en: A shear transform offsets one axis in proportion to the other, producing a slanted silhouette.

distinction.zh: 倾斜通过切变改变元素形状；位移沿坐标轴移动元素。
distinction.en: Skew shears an element's shape; Translate moves it along an axis.

definition.zh: 在三维空间中通过 rotateX 或 rotateY 旋转，增加纵深感。
definition.en: Rotation around the X or Y axis creates a plane-turning effect within a perspective scene.

definition.zh: 控制 3D 效果的强弱；较小的值会夸大纵深，像观察者距离更近。
definition.en: Perspective sets the viewer-to-plane distance used to project three-dimensional transforms; shorter distances amplify depth.

distinction.zh: 透视控制 3D 投影的纵深强度；位移控制元素的空间位置。
distinction.en: Perspective controls the depth of a 3D projection; Translate controls spatial position.

definition.zh: 缩放展开或旋转所围绕的锚点。
definition.en: The transform origin defines the local pivot from which scaling and rotation are calculated.

distinction.zh: 变换原点是缩放或旋转的锚点；纯位移不会因变换原点不同而改变轨迹。
distinction.en: Transform origin anchors scale or rotation; a pure translation keeps the same path across origins.

definition.zh: 元素从触发它的控件位置展开，例如弹出层从打开它的按钮处生长，而非沿用 CSS 默认的自身中心。
definition.en: Entrance geometry begins at the control or location that triggered the new surface, preserving their spatial relationship.

definition.zh: 一个元素淡出的同时，另一个元素在同一位置淡入。
definition.en: Two overlapping states exchange opacity over the same interval, handing visual focus from one to the other.

definition.zh: 通过视觉连接变化前后的状态来帮助用户保持方位感，例如让同一个矩形放大或缩小。
definition.en: A transition preserves recognizable geometry or position across states so viewers can follow the change.

distinction.zh: 连续性过渡是一条保持前后关联的设计原则；形变是实现连续性的具体方式之一。
distinction.en: Continuity transition is a principle for preserving before-and-after relationships; Morph is one technique that can create continuity.

definition.zh: 一个形状平滑地变成另一个形状，例如灵动岛。
definition.en: Interpolated geometry reshapes one visual form into another across a continuous transition.

definition.zh: 同一个元素从一个位置移动并变换到另一个位置，例如缩略图展开成卡片。
definition.en: A persistent visual object changes position, size, or styling while connecting two layouts.

distinction.zh: 共享元素过渡让同一元素跨布局移动并变换；形变强调一个形状变成另一个形状。
distinction.en: Shared element transition moves and transforms the same element across layouts; Morph emphasizes one shape becoming another.

definition.zh: 元素尺寸或位置变化时，以动画移动到新状态，而非瞬间跳变。
definition.en: Changes in measured position or dimensions are animated between layout snapshots.

distinction.zh: 布局动画把尺寸或位置变化平滑化；形变改变元素的形状。
distinction.en: Layout animation smooths size or position changes; Morph changes an element's shape.

definition.zh: 一个区域平滑地展开或收起高度，以显示或隐藏内容。
definition.en: A disclosure region animates its visible extent while content is opened or closed.

definition.zh: 前进时内容沿一个方向滑动，返回时沿相反方向滑动，让导航具备方向感。
definition.en: Navigation motion uses the route direction to choose opposing travel paths for forward and reverse actions.

definition.zh: 元素进入视口时淡入或滑动到位。
definition.en: Viewport entry triggers a one-time opacity or position transition for newly visible content.

definition.zh: 动画进度与滚动位置直接绑定。
definition.en: Scroll progress supplies the animation timeline, mapping page movement to visual state.

definition.zh: 滚动时背景与前景以不同速度移动，从而营造纵深感。
definition.en: Layers respond to the same scroll input at different displacement rates, creating apparent depth.

definition.zh: 从一个页面或路由导航到另一个页面或路由时播放的动画。
definition.en: Route navigation is accompanied by coordinated exit and entrance states for page-level content.

definition.zh: 浏览器在两个状态或页面之间进行形变过渡，并连接共享元素。
definition.en: The browser captures old and new render states, then animates their snapshots and designated shared elements.

distinction.zh: 视图过渡指浏览器连接两个 DOM 状态或页面快照的机制；页面过渡涵盖更广泛的路由切换动画。
distinction.en: View transition refers to the browser connecting DOM states or page snapshots; Page transition covers route-change animation more broadly.

definition.zh: 光标移到元素上方时产生的视觉变化。
definition.en: Pointer hover updates a control's visual state to signal availability or affordance.

definition.zh: 元素被点击时轻微缩小，让操作具有实体触感。
definition.en: A brief response during pointer or touch activation makes a control acknowledge contact.

definition.zh: 用户持续按住按钮时逐步填满的进度效果。
definition.en: A timed progress indicator advances while continuous press input is maintained and completes at a threshold.

definition.zh: 抓住元素并移动它，释放时通常带有动量。
definition.en: Pointer movement directly updates an object's position from grab until release.

distinction.zh: 拖拽是抓取并移动元素的通用手势；拖拽排序把该手势用于列表重排。
distinction.en: Drag is the general gesture of grabbing and moving an element; Drag to reorder applies it to list ordering.

definition.zh: 拖动列表项来重新排序，其他项目会移动并让出位置。
definition.en: Dragging an item changes its list position while surrounding items animate toward their prospective slots.

definition.zh: 把元素拖出屏幕以关闭它，例如抽屉或提示消息。
definition.en: A directional drag removes a surface after distance or velocity crosses a dismissal threshold.

definition.zh: 拖过边界时出现阻力并回弹，类似 iOS 的过度滚动手感。
definition.en: Movement beyond a boundary is attenuated, then restored toward the valid range after release.

distinction.zh: 橡皮筋回弹发生在拖过边界后，并带阻力返回；滑动关闭以越过阈值后移除元素为目标。
distinction.en: Rubber-banding resists and returns after a boundary is crossed; Swipe to dismiss removes an element after a threshold.

definition.zh: 快速左右抖动，用来提示错误或输入被拒绝。
definition.en: A rapid alternating displacement flags invalid input or a rejected action.

definition.zh: 圆形从轻触点向外扩散，用来确认按压操作。
definition.en: A radial wave expands from the activation coordinates to mark where contact occurred.

definition.zh: 动画加速或减速的速率变化方式。
definition.en: An easing function maps linear timeline progress to a changing rate of property interpolation.

definition.zh: 开始快、结束慢，适合作为大多数 UI 动效和用户响应动作的默认选择。
definition.en: Progress advances quickly after activation and decelerates near completion, giving responsive UI motion a controlled arrival.

distinction.zh: 缓出是缓动的一种：开始快、结束慢，适合响应用户输入。
distinction.en: Ease-out is an easing profile that starts fast and ends slowly, fitting user-triggered responses.

definition.zh: 开始慢、结束快，通常应谨慎使用，因为容易显得迟缓。
definition.en: Progress accelerates from a gentle start toward a faster finish, fitting departures that should gather speed.

distinction.zh: 缓入是缓动的一种：开始慢、结束快，容易让 UI 响应显得迟缓。
distinction.en: Ease-in is an easing profile that starts slowly and ends fast, which can make UI response feel sluggish.

definition.zh: 速度由慢到快再到慢，适合屏幕上已有元素从 A 点移动到 B 点。
definition.en: Progress accelerates through the middle and decelerates at both ends, supporting visible point-to-point travel.

distinction.zh: 缓入缓出两端较慢、中段较快，适合屏幕上已有元素的位移。
distinction.en: Ease-in-out is slow at both ends and faster in the middle, fitting movement of elements already on screen.

definition.zh: 保持恒定速度；UI 动效应避免使用，仅保留给加载旋转器或跑马灯。
definition.en: Timeline progress and property progress remain proportional, producing a constant rate suitable for continuous indicators.

distinction.zh: 线性缓动保持恒定速度，适合旋转器和跑马灯等连续运动。
distinction.en: Linear easing keeps constant speed, fitting continuous motion such as spinners and marquees.

definition.zh: 由开发者定义的自定义缓动曲线，用于精确控制速度变化。
definition.en: Four control points describe a cubic Bézier timing curve for custom acceleration and deceleration.

distinction.zh: 三次贝塞尔是自定义缓动曲线的表达方式，可精确控制加速与减速。
distinction.en: Cubic-bezier expresses a custom easing curve for precise acceleration and deceleration control.

definition.zh: 加速与减速速率不同的曲线，通常比对称曲线更有生命力。
definition.en: Different acceleration and deceleration shapes give the entrance and arrival distinct timing characteristics.

distinction.zh: 非对称缓动使用不同的加速与减速速率；它属于自定义缓动策略。
distinction.en: Asymmetric easing uses different acceleration and deceleration rates; it is a custom easing strategy.

definition.zh: 由张力、质量和阻尼等物理参数驱动的运动，不依赖固定时长。
definition.en: A simulated oscillator approaches a target according to stiffness, damping, mass, and current velocity.

definition.zh: 弹簧拉向目标的力度，数值越高，响应越干脆。
definition.en: Stiffness determines the restoring force applied for a given distance from the spring's target.

distinction.zh: 刚度 / 张力决定弹簧拉向目标的力度，是弹簧模型的参数。
distinction.en: Stiffness / Tension controls how strongly a spring pulls toward its target; it is a spring-model parameter.

definition.zh: 弹簧稳定下来的速度；阻尼越低，回弹和振荡越明显。
definition.en: Damping removes energy from a spring over time, controlling oscillation and settlement.

distinction.zh: 阻尼决定弹簧消除振荡的速度；弹簧是包含阻尼在内的完整物理模型。
distinction.en: Damping controls how quickly oscillation settles; Spring is the full physical model that includes damping.

definition.zh: 动画元素呈现出的重量；质量越大，运动越慢、越沉。
definition.en: Mass sets inertia in the spring equation, influencing acceleration and the perceived weight of motion.

distinction.zh: 质量决定元素呈现的重量与惯性；它与刚度、阻尼共同塑造弹簧响应。
distinction.en: Mass controls perceived weight and inertia; it works with stiffness and damping to shape a spring response.

definition.zh: 弹簧越过目标后再稳定下来，为动作增加活泼感。
definition.en: A spring crosses its target one or more times before its oscillation converges.

distinction.zh: 弹跳是弹簧越过目标后回落的表现，通常由较低阻尼和合适刚度产生。
distinction.en: Bounce is a spring overshooting and settling, commonly produced by lower damping with suitable stiffness.

definition.zh: 弹簧在感知上完成所需的时长，尽管底层仍在进行微小的收敛。
definition.en: The perceived duration ends when remaining spring movement falls below a visually meaningful threshold.

distinction.zh: 感知时长描述用户觉得弹簧已经结束的时刻；它区别于固定 CSS 时长。
distinction.en: Perceptual duration describes when a spring feels finished to the user; it differs from a fixed CSS duration.

definition.zh: 保留速度并继续前进的运动，常见于拖拽释放或动画中断之后。
definition.en: Stored velocity continues to influence motion after direct manipulation ends.

distinction.zh: 动量让动作在拖拽释放或中断后保留速度；弹簧可以接收并延续这份速度。
distinction.en: Momentum preserves velocity after release or interruption; a spring can receive and carry that velocity.

definition.zh: 元素移动的快慢与方向；弹簧会在中断时把它带入下一段动画，使被快速甩动的元素保留速度。
definition.en: Velocity records both speed and direction, allowing a new transition to inherit current movement.

distinction.zh: 速度记录运动的快慢与方向；弹簧把它作为下一段响应的初始条件。
distinction.en: Velocity records speed and direction; a spring can use it as the initial condition for the next response.

definition.zh: 动画可以在运行途中平滑地改变目标，无需等待当前动作完成。
definition.en: An in-progress transition accepts a new target while retaining continuity of position and velocity.

distinction.zh: 可打断动画描述运行中可平滑改向的交互能力；弹簧是实现这项能力的一种方式。
distinction.en: Interruptible animation names the ability to retarget motion smoothly in flight; a spring is one way to implement it.

definition.zh: 文字或内容持续循环滚动。
definition.en: A content track translates continuously and wraps to create an uninterrupted scrolling strip.

definition.zh: 按指定次数或无限重复播放的动画。
definition.en: A timeline restarts according to a finite iteration count or continues indefinitely.

definition.zh: 每轮先正向播放再反向播放的循环，避免直接跳回起点。
definition.en: Successive iterations reverse playback direction so each endpoint becomes the next starting point.

distinction.zh: 往返循环每轮反向播放；循环也可以采用正向、反向或有限次数等其他策略。
distinction.en: Alternate reverses direction each iteration; Loop also includes normal, reverse, finite, and infinite strategies.

definition.zh: 一个元素沿连续路径围绕另一个元素旋转。
definition.en: An element follows a closed path around a center or companion object.

definition.zh: 通过轻柔重复的缩放或透明度变化吸引注意。
definition.en: A low-amplitude cycle of scale, opacity, or color periodically emphasizes an element.

distinction.zh: 脉冲通过重复缩放或透明度变化吸引注意；空闲动效是包含脉冲在内的更宽泛类别。
distinction.en: Pulse repeats scale or opacity changes to attract attention; Idle animation is the broader category that can include it.

definition.zh: 轻柔、持续的上下漂移，让静态元素显得有生命力且轻盈。
definition.en: A slow repeating translation produces a light hovering impression around a stable resting point.

distinction.zh: 漂浮持续进行轻微上下位移；空闲动效也可以使用脉冲等其他低强度动作。
distinction.en: Float uses continuous subtle vertical drift; Idle animation can also use pulse and other low-intensity motion.

definition.zh: 元素静置并等待交互时播放的细微运动。
definition.en: Low-intensity ambient motion gives a waiting element activity without requiring input.

definition.zh: 用于柔化元素或掩盖细小瑕疵的模糊滤镜。
definition.en: A blur filter blends neighboring pixels to soften edges, depth layers, or transitional artifacts.

definition.zh: 把元素裁切成指定形状，可用于揭示、遮罩和前后对比滑杆。
definition.en: A geometric clipping region controls which portion of an element is painted.

distinction.zh: 裁切路径使用清晰形状边界隐藏区域；模糊滤镜柔化像素，并不定义可见区域。
distinction.en: Clip-path hides regions with a defined shape boundary; Blur softens pixels and does not define the visible region.

definition.zh: 使用形状或渐变隐藏、揭示元素的部分区域；它类似裁切路径，同时支持柔和、可渐隐的边缘。
definition.en: An alpha or luminance image modulates visibility, allowing gradients and soft-edged reveals.

distinction.zh: 遮罩通过透明度或亮度控制可见区域并支持软边；模糊滤镜只负责柔化图像。
distinction.en: Mask controls visible regions through alpha or luminance and supports soft edges; Blur only softens the image.

definition.zh: 通过可拖动分割线在两张叠放图片之间擦拭切换，以便对比。
definition.en: A draggable boundary changes the visible share of two aligned layers for direct comparison.

definition.zh: SVG 路径像被一支隐形画笔描摹一样自行绘制出来。
definition.en: Animating SVG stroke dash values reveals a path progressively along its length.

definition.zh: 文字变化时逐字符播放动画，把注意力引向新值。
definition.en: Characters or glyph shapes transition between text states to focus attention on updated content.

definition.zh: 内容加载期间显示的占位结构，表面带有移动的微光。
definition.en: A placeholder layout uses a moving highlight to communicate that content is still loading.

definition.zh: 数字滚动或递增到目标值。
definition.en: Numeric glyphs translate or interpolate until the display reaches an updated value.

definition.zh: 使用固定宽度的数字，使数值变化时不会左右跳动；它对数字滚动、计时器和计数器非常重要。
definition.en: Tabular numeral glyphs share a fixed advance width, keeping changing values spatially stable.

distinction.zh: 等宽数字是稳定数字宽度的排版特性；数字滚动是数值变化时播放的动画。
distinction.en: Tabular numbers is a typographic feature that stabilizes digit width; Number ticker is the animation used when values change.

definition.zh: 文字像正在输入一样逐字符出现。
definition.en: A text sequence reveals characters at timed intervals to simulate incremental entry.

definition.zh: 每秒绘制的帧数；60fps 是流畅运动的基线，新款显示器可达到 120fps。
definition.en: Frame rate counts rendered frames per second; matching the display refresh cadence produces the smoothest result.

definition.zh: 浏览器无法跟上动画并发生掉帧时出现的可见停顿。
definition.en: Jank is a perceptible break in motion caused by irregular frame timing or delayed main-thread work.

distinction.zh: 卡顿是用户看到的运动停顿；帧率是用于衡量绘制速度的指标。
distinction.en: Jank is the visible stutter a user sees; Frame rate is the metric used to measure drawing speed.

definition.zh: 浏览器未能在截止时间前绘制的一帧，会让运动出现轻微顿挫。
definition.en: A dropped frame occurs when rendering misses a display deadline and the previous image remains visible for another refresh.

distinction.zh: 丢帧是一次未赶上绘制截止时间的事件；帧率汇总一段时间内的绘制频率。
distinction.en: Dropped frame is one missed drawing deadline; Frame rate summarizes drawing frequency over time.

definition.zh: 让 GPU 在独立图层上移动元素或改变其透明度，无需重新执行布局或绘制。
definition.en: Compositing assembles painted layers into the final frame and can update transforms or opacity without repeating layout.

definition.zh: 一种 CSS 提示，用来告知浏览器元素即将播放动画，使浏览器可以提前把它提升到独立图层。
definition.en: The will-change property warns the browser about an upcoming property change so it can prepare relevant rendering resources.

distinction.zh: will-change 是浏览器优化提示；合成是浏览器把图层交给 GPU 处理的渲染阶段。
distinction.en: will-change is an optimization hint; Compositing is the rendering stage that lets the GPU handle layers.

definition.zh: 动画化 width、height、top 或 left 等属性，迫使浏览器逐帧重新计算布局并引发卡顿。
definition.en: Repeated interleaving of layout reads and writes forces synchronous recalculation and consumes the frame budget.

distinction.zh: 布局抖动由反复触发布局计算造成；合成友好的 transform 和 opacity 可以绕开这类工作。
distinction.en: Layout thrashing comes from repeated layout calculation; compositor-friendly transform and opacity can avoid that work.

definition.zh: 动效应承担明确功能，例如帮助定位、提供反馈或展示关系，而非只作装饰。
definition.en: Purposeful motion communicates hierarchy, causality, feedback, or spatial relationships in support of a user task.

definition.zh: 动作开始前先向相反方向进行一小段蓄力，用来预示即将发生的运动。
definition.en: A brief preparatory movement establishes direction and energy before the primary action.

definition.zh: 主运动停止后，元素的部分结构继续移动并稍后稳定，从而增加重量感。
definition.en: Secondary parts continue and settle after the main body reaches its destination, reinforcing weight and flexibility.

distinction.zh: 跟随动作发生在主运动停止之后；预备动作发生在主运动开始之前。
distinction.en: Follow-through happens after the main motion stops; Anticipation happens before the main motion starts.

definition.zh: 在元素运动时改变其形状，用来表现重量、速度和弹性。
definition.en: Temporary deformation links shape to force, speed, or impact while preserving recognizable volume.

distinction.zh: 挤压与拉伸通过形变表达重量和弹性；预备动作通过反向蓄力预示运动。
distinction.en: Squash & stretch uses deformation to express weight and flexibility; Anticipation uses an opposite wind-up to preview motion.

definition.zh: 恰当的动画可以让界面在实际速度未变化时显得更快。
definition.en: Motion and progressive feedback reduce uncertainty during waiting, changing how fast the interface feels.

definition.zh: 用户看到一段动画的频率越高，它就应越短、越克制。
definition.en: Exposure frequency sets an animation's acceptable emphasis and length; repeated motion benefits from greater restraint.

distinction.zh: 使用频率决定动效应多短、多克制；有目的的动效决定它承担什么功能。
distinction.en: Frequency of use determines how short and subtle motion should be; Purposeful animation determines the function it serves.

definition.zh: 让元素在不同状态之间保持身份与空间位置的连续，使用户始终知道内容去了哪里。
definition.en: Objects retain coherent position, direction, and identity across states so changes remain easy to trace.

distinction.zh: 空间一致性保持元素跨状态的身份与位置连续；有目的的动效为这种连续性提供理由。
distinction.en: Spatial consistency preserves identity and position across states; Purposeful animation supplies the reason for that continuity.

definition.zh: 动画化 transform 和 opacity，让 GPU 保持运动流畅。
definition.en: Hardware acceleration uses compositor and GPU resources for eligible visual updates, helping frames meet display deadlines.

distinction.zh: 硬件加速描述利用 GPU 保持动画流畅的策略；合成是浏览器把独立图层交给 GPU 处理的渲染阶段。
distinction.en: Hardware acceleration describes using the GPU to keep motion smooth; Compositing is the rendering stage that hands independent layers to the GPU.

definition.zh: 尊重用户的 prefers-reduced-motion 设置，降低或移除动态效果。
definition.en: Reduced-motion behavior honors the user preference with stable spatial states and concise essential feedback.
